---
title: "Home"
page-layout: full
---

# Nicholas Gladwell

<img src="profile_photo.jpg?v=2"
     width="200"
     loading="eager"
     decoding="sync"
     fetchpriority="high"
     style="display:block; background-color:white;">

I hold a Master's in Pharmacology, with interests in drug discovery, preclinical research, neuropharmacology, and pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics. My work combines experimental laboratory techniques with computational methods to better understand how drugs interact with biological systems from the molecular to the organism level.

---

## Bio
I graduated from Brigham Young University in 2024 with a double major in Exercise Science and Spanish before completing a Master's in Pharmacology at the University of the Basque Country in Bilbao, Spain. My graduate research investigated the effects of psilocybin on brain cellular responses and neuronal plasticity associated with depression using transgenic mouse models, immunohistochemistry, confocal microscopy, and quantitative image analysis.

Through my coursework and research, I have developed interests spanning neuropharmacology, pharmaceutical formulation, pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modeling, and translational drug development. I enjoy integrating laboratory experimentation with computational methods to better understand drug mechanisms and biological responses.

---

## Research Interests
+ Neuropharmacology
+ Pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics (including predictive modeling)
+ Pharmaceutical formulation and drug delivery
+ Preclinical and translational research


---

## Skills and Tools
+ <b>Laboratory techniques:</b> Cell culture, PCR/qPCR, Western blot, brain tissue processing, immunohistochemistry, confocal microscopy
+ <b>Data analysis and modeling:</b> Python, Phoenix WinNonlin, GraphPad Prism
+ <b>Image analysis:</b> FIJI/ImageJ
+ <b>Molecular visualization:</b> PyMOL


---

## Projects

### Structural Basis of Functional Selectivity in 5-HT<sub>2A</sub> and 5-HT<sub>2B</sub> Receptors

Comparative analysis of serotonin receptor subtypes using structural biology and computational visualization to assess ligand binding and receptor activation.

[View Project →](serotonin-receptor-analysis.qmd)

### Monte Carlo Simulation of Antibiotic PK Variability and Probability of Target Attainment

Simulating interindividual pharmacokinetic variability to evaluate the probability of achieving therapeutic antibiotic exposure targets.

[View Project →](pk-monte-carlo-simulation.qmd)
